active_direction.summary: Build full, multi-file SWE-E2E reconstruction tasks from real source repositories or benchmark cases backed by real repositories.
active_direction.retired_direction: The old mini-task and unit/system-gap line is archived and no longer maintained as the active design path.
active_direction.candidate_visibility_rule: Candidate agents may see only the public packet, dependency constraints, starter files if intentionally public, and output instructions.
candidate_must_not_see: oracle fixtures not described by the public packet

evaluation_policy.primary_harness: mini swe agent / OpenHands-backed candidate execution
evaluation_policy.oracle_policy: Prefer filtered original tests and deterministic executable rubrics.
evaluation_policy.baseline_policy: Every promoted task must be tested with at least one weak/contrast model and a fresh Codex subagent SOTA baseline, using isolated public-packet workspaces only.

required_candidate_baselines.runner: Codex subagent in a clean candidate workspace

fairness_gate: requirements are traceable to public packet and source evidence
hidden checks are non-isomorphic to public checklist bullets
no brittle exact formatting unless public contract promises it
network and platform assumptions are mocked, removed, or made deterministic
repeated root causes are reported separately from raw score

skills_policy.purpose: Accumulate reusable procedures while building tasks so team members can repeat the workflow.
skills_policy.promotion_gate: A workflow should become a skill only after it has been exercised on a real candidate and has clear inputs, outputs, gates, and failure modes.
skills_policy.stage_skill_discipline: Before every pipeline stage, read the stage's specialist SKILL.md from skills/{stage-skill}/SKILL.md and treat it as the only workflow authority for that stage. Subagents must be instructed to read the same stage skill before inspecting artifacts.
skills_policy.deprecated_skill_policy: Deprecated root-level and v1 workflow skills are archival only and must not be used as runtime authority.

preferred: GitHub repositories with clear docs and tests
fit_gate: reasonable LOC and file count for multi-file reconstruction
pilot exception: the first task may be below 3000 LOC only to validate the full workflow, but it must still be multi-file and backed by original tests
durable state or shared fact source with derived views
reject_gate: can be solved as a single small file
pure closed-form parser or formatter with no lifecycle
hidden checks require private source details
tests are mostly network-bound, platform-bound, or unclear
task is dominated by famous standard memorization
